semanticCore: Beard, facial whiskers, jawbone, or peeling/barking a tree

keyDerivations.meaning: Beard, facial hair growing on the chin and jaws

figurativeOrRareUsages: قَالَ يَا ابْنَ أُمَّ لَا تَأْخُذْ بِلِحْيَتِي وَلَا بِرَأْسِي (20:94): '[Aaron] said: O son of my mother, do not seize [me] by my beard nor by my head!'
summaryEn: The root represents the beard (Lihyah), recording Aaron's plead to Moses not to pull his beard in anger over the golden calf incident.
summaryAr: يشير هذا الجذر إلى شعر اللحية النابت على الذقن والخدين، وقشر الشجرة (لحي)، ملتمساً هارون من أخيه موسى أن يترك رأسه ولحيته غضباً.
rawText: اِلْتَحَيْتُ اللَّحْمَ عَنِ العَظْمِ 8 [I peeled off the flesh from the bone]. (O, K, in art. سلق.) تَلَحَّى 5 , (S, Mgh,) and تلحّى بِعِمَامَتِهِ, (TA, art. عجر,) He wound a part of his turban under his lower jaw: (S, Mgh:) as also تَحَنَّكَ, q. v. لَحْىٌ [not لَحًى] The jaw-bone; (Mgh, Msb;) either upper or lower: (Msb:) and, in a man, (Msb,) the part on which the beard grows. (S, Msb, K.) === And sometimes Either lateral portion of the lower jaw: see ذَقَنٌ, and الفَنِيكُ. لَوَاحِى الشَّيْبِ , for لَوَائِحُ الشيب, q. v. لُحِىَ 1 : see a verse cited voce خَطَبَ. === لَحَاهُ ٱللّٰهُ: see شَرَاهُ. لِحَآءٌ Bark; and in particular the bark of a plant, or of a tree, of which ropes &c. are made. === The لِحَآء of the date is [its Pulp, pulpy pericarp, or flesh; i. e.] the part that clothes the نَوَاة [or stone]. (TA.) === لِحَآءُ العِنَبِ [The skin of grapes]. (TA in art. شحم.) لِحْيَةُ التَّيْسِ : see ثِيلٌ. === لِحْيَةٌ is sometimes, by a synecdoche, put for the whole person: see a verse cited voce خُنْثٌ. مَسْحُ اللُِّحَى : see عَقَّ بِالسَّهْمِ.
